source: Colonial Development Fund Schemes quarterly progress reports and financial statements__CO273:655:7
filename: Colonial Development Fund Schemes quarterly progress reports and financial statements__CO273:655:7.txt
summary:
- **Core purpose/topic:** Colonial Office handling of **Colonial Development Fund (C.D.F.) Scheme No. 31** (“**Nutrition Problems in Native Diet**” / **Vitamin Research**) including **quarterly progress reports** and **audited financial statements**, and resolving how expenditure should be charged (capital vs recurrent) and any over-issue recovery.  
- **Geography & institutions:** **Straits Settlements**, centred on the **College of Medicine, Singapore**; involvement of the **Straits Settlements Government**, **Colonial Office**, **Director of Colonial Audit**, and **Crown Agents for the Colonies**.  
- **Time period covered:** Research began **1 Nov 1930**; grant assistance ceased **3 May 1939** (scheme period extended to that date); reporting and audit correspondence mainly **1939–1940** (e.g., despatch **28 Dec 1939**, audit certification **5 Jan 1940**, Colonial Office letter **Feb 1940**).  
- **Scientific work reported (Apr–Jun 1939):** Concluded study of **vitamin A in Malayan bananas** (published **Sept**, Journal of Malaya Branch BMA vol. 3 p.156); advanced **vitamin C assays** using guinea-pig tests comparing reduced/reversibly oxidised/irreversibly oxidised ascorbic acid (finding the first two equal in antiscorbutic potency; irreversibly oxidised inactive) and began food surveys using **indophenol titration**; continued dietetic survey of **17 foods** and trace element analyses (incl. aluminium, cobalt, nickel, zinc).  
- **Other research findings noted:** **Basal metabolism** study of **Malay schoolboys** concluded (published **Sept**, Journal of Malaya Branch BMA vol. 3 p.143), reporting no significant difference from temperate-climate standards; first-quarter 1939 report notes **36 subjects** studied and methodological work converting vitamin values to **International Units**.  
- **Key financial figures/decisions:** Total C.D.F. grant **£6,000** (intended **£3,000 capital + £3,000 recurrent**). Audited expenditure to **3 May 1939** shown as **capital £2,754 0s 4d** and **recurrent £3,245 19s 8d** (total £6,000). Colonial Office determined only **£5,754 0s 4d** was chargeable to the Fund (recurrent capped at £3,000), creating an **excess/over-issue of £245 19s 8d**.  
- **Recovery action:** The **£245 19s 8d** excess was **recovered on 27 Feb 1940** via the **Crown Agents**, from funds held for the **Government of the Straits Settlements**, and credited back to the Colonial Development Fund.
